content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

January 19, 1989

Ms. Betty Presley
1251 E. Dyer Road
Suite 100
Santa Ana, CA 92705

Dear Betty:                                    T-89-041

     This letter is to confirm the conclusion of our telephone
conversation on January 19, 1989. You wanted confirmation
regarding the Proposition 73 limits on receipt of contributions
by political committees for purposes other than contributing to
candidates. Government Code Section 85303(c) provides:

     Nothing in this Chapter shall limit a person's ability
     to provide financial support to one or more political
     committees or broad based political committees provided
     that the support is used for purposes other than making
     contributions directly to candidates for elective
     office.

     The Commission staff has been recommending that political
committees may want to create separate bank accounts for receipt
of funds which will be used for making contributions to candidates
and for funds which are not used for making contributions to
candidates.

     If you have further questions, you may call me at (916) 322-
5662.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Bruce W. Robeck
                                        Political Reform Consultant


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

sections.conclusion: Nothing in this Chapter shall limit a person's ability to provide financial support to one or more political committees or broad based political committees provided that the support is used for purposes other than making contributions directly to candidates for elective office.
sections.question_synthetic: Are there limits under Proposition 73 on contributions received by political committees for purposes other than contributing to candidates?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Government Code Section 85303(c) provides that there are no limits on financial support to political committees when such support is used for purposes other than making contributions to candidates. The FPPC staff recommends that political committees create separate bank accounts to distinguish funds used for candidate contributions from those used for other purposes.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Informal advice letter format based on telephone conversation; no formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections present but legal guidance clearly provided; date discrepancy shows 'January 19, 1989' in body but document ID is T-89-041.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Are there limits under Proposition 73 on contributions received by political committees for purposes other than contributing to candidates?

CONCLUSION: Nothing in this Chapter shall limit a person's ability to provide financial support to one or more political committees or broad based political committees provided that the support is used for purposes other than making contributions directly to candidates for elective office.

Dyer Road Suite 100 Santa Ana, CA 92705 Dear Betty: T-89-041 This letter is to confirm the conclusion of our telephone conversation on January 19, 1989. You wanted confirmation regarding the Proposition 73 limits on receipt of contributions by political committees for purposes other than contributing to candidates. Government Code Section 85303(c) provides: Nothing in this Chapter shall limit a person's ability to provide financial support to one or more political committees or broad based political committees provided that the support is used for purposes other than making contributions directly to candidates for elective office. The Commission staff has been recommending that political committees may want to create separate bank accounts for receipt of funds which will be used for making contributions to candidates and for funds which are not used for making contributions to candidates. If you have further questions, you may call me at (916) 322- 5662. Sincerely, Bruce W. Robeck Political Reform Consultant 428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660
embedding.summary: FPPC advice letter confirming that Proposition 73 does not limit contributions to political committees when funds are used for purposes other than contributing to candidates. The Commission staff recommends using separate bank accounts to track these different fund types.
